title: Rework the drifted execute-path batch with machine-checkable completion markers

stories.title: Pin the onboarding contract to one exact controller-stage/result payload
stories.scope: Narrow the rework to one exact contract path only. In `OneOPS/app/device/v2/api/device_v2_onboarding_api.go`, the single-device onboarding response for `GET /device/v2/:code/onboarding` and `POST /device/v2/:code/onboarding/ensure` must always expose one `monitor_controller_stage` action whose evidence carries the exact controller-backed fields already implied by the code path: `controller_backed`, `controller_stage`, `store_run_status`, `core_store_status`, `manageable_status`, `task_id`, and `store_run_id`. In `OneOPS-UI/src/views/device/DeviceV2ManagementRedesign.vue`, the continue-onboarding result area must read and visibly present that same controller stage/result combination instead of falling back to generic message-only wording. If one of those exact fields cannot be produced or surfaced, return BLOCKED with the exact file/function/missing-field blocker rather than a broad summary.
nonGoals: Do not close the story with docs-only or evidence-only edits.
Do not widen scope beyond `device_v2_onboarding_api.go`, `device-v2.ts`, and the continue-onboarding UI presentation in `DeviceV2ManagementRedesign.vue` unless the exact contract path forces it.
Do not fabricate controller success when `controller_stage` or the controller-backed result fields are absent or empty.
acceptance: The onboarding API returns at least one `monitor_controller_stage` action whose evidence includes `controller_backed`, `controller_stage`, `store_run_status`, `core_store_status`, `manageable_status`, `task_id`, and `store_run_id` whenever a latest onboarding run exists.
The continue-onboarding UI visibly renders the exact controller stage/result combination from that action instead of reducing the result to generic success/blocker copy alone.

A durable evidence file `docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-023.md` is written with `Verdict: done-with-code` on DONE, or with `Verdict: exact-contract-blocker` plus the exact `file`, `function`, and `missing field` when BLOCKED.
notes: Rework target: D2ON-020 drifted into doc-only completion and must not be treated as a valid completion pattern.
If blocked, the evidence file must spell the blocker as `file=<path>`, `function=<name>`, `missing=<field or UI slot>`.
Not enough for DONE: changing only docs, changing only evidence wording, or saying the next story can do the real work.

stories.title: Probe one exact continue-onboarding entrypoint and capture the returned stage verdict
stories.scope: After D2ON-023 lands, use exactly one entrypoint for the live probe: the single-device continue-onboarding flow that calls `planDeviceV2OnboardingReq` and then `ensureDeviceV2OnboardingReq` from `DeviceV2ManagementRedesign.vue`. A DONE result is valid only if `docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-024.md` records the exact device code, monitor mode if any, whether the returned action was `monitor_controller_stage`, and the exact returned values for `controller_stage`, `store_run_status`, `core_store_status`, and `manageable_status`, or an exact blocker naming which one was missing. Do not close on a generic statement that real execution evidence is unavailable.
nonGoals: Do not perform batch onboarding execution.
Do not hide the exact stage fields behind generic success/failure wording.
Do not treat local contract persistence as the real execution result.
acceptance: A durable evidence file `docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-024.md` records the exact request path `plan -> ensure`, device code, monitor mode, and the returned `monitor_controller_stage` action payload or exact absence.
The evidence file must contain `Verdict: controller-trace` or `Verdict: exact-blocker`.

Any retryable, failed, blocked, or unknown outcome is preserved explicitly with the exact returned `controller_stage`, `store_run_status`, `core_store_status`, and `manageable_status` values rather than rewritten as success.
notes: Rework target: D2ON-021 again drifted into doc-only completion and did not yield a controller-backed trace or exact blocker.
Must read first: docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-018.md, docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-023.md, and the exact code/evidence touched by D2ON-023.
The evidence should quote the exact payload fields returned from the onboarding API, not only a prose conclusion.
Not enough for DONE: repeating that real execution evidence is unavailable without naming the exact stage and blocker.

stories.title: Rework readiness with an explicit program verdict after the real execution result
stories.scope: Update readiness and review only after D2ON-024 produces a machine-checkable real execution verdict. The readiness result must explicitly state whether the bounded task is now high-quality complete or whether another execution rework is required. Do not close on another passive wait note.
nonGoals: Do not close the whole program unless the real execution result truly justifies it.
Do not restate old passive waiting language.
Do not skip the explicit quality verdict.
acceptance: final-readiness.md and review.md are updated from the new real execution result rather than from passive waiting.
A durable evidence file `docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-025.md` is written with `ProgramVerdict: high-quality-complete` or `ProgramVerdict: further-execution-rework-needed`.
The next-step note explicitly states whether another execution rework batch is required.
notes: Rework target: D2ON-022 closed the batch without a trustworthy quality verdict on the real execution task.
Not enough for DONE: another passive wait conclusion or another formal completion note that does not classify the real task quality.
